heading: Abandoned mine reclamation fund authorization of appropriations
label: 1231a Abandoned mine reclamation fund authorization of appropriations

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated, for deposit into the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund established by section 401(a) of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 () $11,293,000,000 for fiscal year 2022, to remain available until expended.30 U.S.C. 1231(a)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (g), amounts made available under subsection (a) shall be used to provide, as expeditiously as practicable, to States and Indian Tribes described in paragraph (2) annual grants for abandoned mine land and water reclamation projects under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 ( et seq.).30 U.S.C. 1201

children.label: A
children.text: States and Indian Tribes that have a State or Tribal program approved under section 405 of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 ();30 U.S.C. 1235

children.label: B
children.text: States and Indian Tribes that are certified under section 411(a) of that Act (); and30 U.S.C. 1240a(a)

children.label: C
children.text: States and Indian Tribes that are referred to in section 402(g)(8)(B) of that Act ().30 U.S.C. 1232(g)(8)(B)

children.heading: Contract aggregation
children.label: 3 Contract aggregation
children.text: In applying for grants under paragraph (1), States and Indian Tribes may aggregate bids into larger statewide or regional contracts.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), grants under subsection (b)(1) shall only be used for activities described in subsections (a) and (b) of section 403 and section 410 of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 (, 1240).30 U.S.C. 1233

children.label: i
children.text: the abatement of the causes and the treatment of the effects of acid mine drainage resulting from coal mining practices, including for the costs of building, operating, maintaining, and rehabilitating acid mine drainage treatment systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: the prevention, abatement, and control of subsidence; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the prevention, abatement, and control of coal mine fires.

children.label: I
children.text: acid mine drainage abatement and treatment;

children.label: II
children.text: subsidence prevention, abatement, and control; and

children.label: III
children.text: coal mine fire prevention, abatement, and control; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include in the annual grant report of the recipient information on the status and balance of amounts in the long-term abandoned mine land reclamation fund.

children.label: i
children.text: subsection (d)(4)(B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: any other limitation on the length of the term of an annual grant under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (e), the Secretary of the Interior shall allocate and distribute amounts made available for grants under subsection (b)(1) to States and Indian Tribes on an equal annual basis over a 15-year period beginning on , based on the number of tons of coal historically produced in the States or from the applicable Indian land before , regardless of whether the State or Indian Tribe is certified under section 411(a) of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 ().November 15, 2021August 3, 197730 U.S.C. 1240a(a)

children.heading: Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act exception
children.label: 2 Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act exception
children.text: Section 401(f)(3)(B) of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 () shall not apply to grant funds distributed under subsection (b)(1).30 U.S.C. 1231(f)(3)(B)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 6 years after the date on which the first allocation to States and Indian Tribes is made under paragraph (1), the Secretary of the Interior shall submit to Congress a report that describes any progress made under this section in addressing outstanding reclamation needs under subsection (a) or (b) of section 403 or section 410 of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation and Act of 1977 (, 1240).130 U.S.C. 1233So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.1

children.label: i
children.text: prior to submitting the report under subparagraph (A), solicit the input of the States and Indian Tribes regarding the progress referred to in that subparagraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include in the report submitted to Congress under that subparagraph a description of any input received under clause (i).

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: A Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 20 years after , the Secretary of the Interior shall evaluate grant payments to States and Indian Tribes made under this section.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Unused funds
children.label: B Unused funds
children.text: On completion of the evaluation under subparagraph (A), States and Indian Tribes shall return any unused funds under this section to the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund.

children.heading: Total amount of grant
children.label: e Total amount of grant
children.text: The total amount of grant funding provided under subsection (b)(1) to an eligible State or Indian Tribe shall be not less than $20,000,000, to the extent that the amount needed for reclamation projects described in that subsection on the land of the State or Indian Tribe is not less than $20,000,000.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: f Priority
children.text: In addition to the priorities described in section 403(a) of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 (), in providing grants under this section, priority may also be given to reclamation projects described in subsection (b)(1) that provide employment for current and former employees of the coal industry.30 U.S.C. 1233(a)

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: g Reservation
children.text: Of the funds made available under subsection (a), $25,000,000 shall be made available to the Secretary of the Interior to provide States and Indian Tribes with the financial and technical assistance necessary for the purpose of making amendments to the inventory maintained under section 403(c) of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 ().30 U.S.C. 1233(c)
